summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on May 3, 2022, addressing three zoning cases. ZC 2022-003 involved Ryan Homes requesting a major adjustment to the Coves of Barclay Woods PUD to allow a 1,366 sq ft ranch home (below the 1,400 sq ft minimum); the commission denied the request. ZC 2022-004 involved Grand Communities proposing a 117-unit subdivision on Curbert Road, which was approved with staff recommendations despite significant public opposition regarding traffic, drainage, and property impacts. ZC 2022-005 involved Grand Communities proposing a 310-lot multi-generational community on Linton Road with single-family, two-family, and duplex products, which generated substantial public concern about density, traffic near schools, and loss of rural character.
decisions: ZC 2021-01 and ZC 2022-02 previously approved by Board of Trustees (old business confirmation)
Minutes from previous meeting approved (with one abstention from Mr. Denwiddie)

ZC 2022-003 (Coves of Barclay Woods / Ryan Homes 1,366 sq ft ranch): Motion to approve with cap of 6 units failed for lack of second; motion to continue to next meeting failed for lack of second; motion to deny the proposed major adjustment passed (Curry aye, Denwiddie yes, Lewis yes, Settle yes)
ZC 2022-004 (Curbert Road / Grand Communities 117-unit subdivision): Approved with staff recommendations (Curry aye, Denwiddie yes, Lewis yes, Settle yes)
ZC 2022-005 (Linton Road / Grand Communities 310-lot subdivision): Discussion held, transcript truncated before final vote

notes: Transcript was truncated before discussion of ZC 2022-005 (Linton Road) was completed, so final vote on that case is unknown. The Linton Road project proposed lot sizes (4,000 sq ft) and lot widths (34 ft for duplexes) that did not meet PUD minimum requirements, requiring relief. The applicant for Curbert Road referenced a contract dispute involving the deceased property owner (Bob Roke) and family members potentially contesting the sale. Staff made a strong appeal for public residents to join the zoning commission, noting vacant seats. Harry Holbert repeatedly emphasized that traffic and stormwater issues are county jurisdiction, not township. The previous zoning case involving a product under 1,400 sq ft was denied by trustees, establishing a clear precedent that influenced the denial of the Ryan Homes request. Fisher Homes founder Henry Fisher was noted as being born in Clermont County.
title: Goshen Zoning Mtg 5.3.2022
